what_it_does: This file implements a browser-global widget registry (window.MS_REGISTRY) and a React hook (window.useRegistry). It lets callers register declarative widget specs, query/list them by category or tag, subscribe to catalog changes, and validate/coerce instance props against each widget's schema.
observations: Uses an IIFE to create a private Map of widgets and a Set of subscribers.
Exposes two globals: window.MS_REGISTRY and window.useRegistry.
Defines a PROP_TYPES whitelist of ten schema types.
normalise() fills in defaults for every field of a widget spec.
register() stores the spec and calls propagate() to notify all subscribers.
list() supports optional category and tag filters and sorts by title.
validate() coerces each schema field and passes through any extra props unchanged.
coerceValue() handles number, boolean, array, enum, and falls back to defaults.
useRegistry() subscribes to registry changes and forces a re-render via React state.
standouts: Declarative widget spec format with schema, emits, render, and tree fields.
Pub/sub propagation so palette/intake/menu consumers update automatically.
Built-in schema validation and type coercion for widget instances.
React hook that auto-re-renders when the registry changes.
conventions: IIFE module pattern with private state via closure.
Global window exports for cross-file access without imports.
concerns: Pollutes the global window namespace with MS_REGISTRY and useRegistry.
validate() forwards extra props without schema checking, which may hide mismatches.
coerceValue() for enum silently falls back to the first option on invalid input.
No handling of duplicate registrations or version conflicts.
useRegistry() re-renders on every registry mutation, which may be expensive.
Assumes React is globally available without import.
notes: A small, self-contained registry module that turns widget specs into a live, queryable, validated catalog and wires it to React via a global hook.
questions: How do consumers choose between render and tree when both are present?
Where are the initial widget specs registered at app startup?
Is React guaranteed to be globally available in all runtime contexts?
How are the emits declarations consumed by the rest of the system?
What is the exact shape of an atomic node spec in the tree field?
Is widget registration persisted across sessions?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a declarative registry with propagation, querying, and validation.
apparent_themes: Runtime extensibility and live catalog updates
Support for both hand-authored and generated artifacts
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a single source of truth for widgets so that palettes, intake flows, menus, and other consumers stay synchronized without manual import wiring, while also giving generated widgets a well-defined contract to plug into.
novelty: The combination of a declarative widget spec, live subscription propagation, built-in schema validation/coercion, and a dedicated React hook in one file-level module.
contribution: Provides a reusable registry abstraction with registration, querying, filtering, pub/sub notification, schema validation, type coercion, and a React hook that keeps UI consumers synchronized with the widget catalog.
summary_for_embedding: A browser-global widget registry module that stores declarative widget specs, notifies subscribers on changes, validates and coerces props against schemas, and exposes a React hook for reactive catalog access.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to let hand-authored or agent-generated widgets register themselves once and become available everywhere in the application through a shared, reactive catalog.

symbols.does: Fills missing fields of a widget spec with sensible defaults and records a registration timestamp.
symbols.description: Takes a raw widget spec and returns a normalized object with guaranteed fields such as version, title, icon, category, schema, emits, render, tree, tags, and registeredAt, ensuring downstream consumers can rely on a consistent shape.

symbols.does: Stores a normalized widget spec under its type key and notifies subscribers.
symbols.description: Validates that the spec has a type, normalizes it, inserts it into the widgets Map, triggers propagation to all subscribers, and returns the stored spec.

symbols.does: Registers an array of widget specs in sequence.
symbols.description: Iterates over the provided array and calls register for each spec, providing a batch registration convenience.

symbols.does: Retrieves a single widget spec by type, or null if absent.
symbols.description: Looks up the given type in the widgets Map and returns the normalized spec, defaulting to null when not found.

symbols.does: Returns all registered widgets, optionally filtered by category or tag, sorted by title.
symbols.description: Collects widget values into an array, applies optional category and tag filters, and sorts alphabetically by title for stable presentation.

symbols.does: Returns the distinct set of category strings currently in use.
symbols.description: Extracts the category field from every registered widget and returns a deduplicated array of category names.

symbols.does: Adds a callback to receive registry updates and returns an unsubscribe function.
symbols.description: Registers a subscriber function in the subs Set; the returned function removes it, enabling safe cleanup by consumers.

symbols.does: Pushes the current widget list to every subscriber.
symbols.description: Builds the current filtered list and calls each subscriber with it, catching and ignoring any thrown errors to protect other subscribers.

symbols.does: Converts a raw prop value to the type expected by a schema definition.
symbols.description: Handles null/empty defaults and switches on schema type to coerce numbers, booleans, arrays, and enums, falling back to the schema default when conversion fails or is unspecified.

symbols.does: Checks instance props against a widget's schema and returns coerced values plus errors.
symbols.description: Fetches the widget spec, coerces each defined schema field, records required-field errors, passes through any extra props not in the schema, and returns an ok flag, error list, and coerced value object.
